lines.text: Our new layout engine brings professional-grade, multi-column
typesetting directly into the automated document pipeline.
typographic fidelity is maintained across all
scripts, locales, and output formats throughout the
Each rubrique de service is composited in its native
locale, letting the engine demonstrate its multilingual
shaping, kerning, and bidirectional text capabilities
This is the start of our two-column layout. When you
work with advanced automated layouts, handling
multi-column text that effortlessly snaps around
floating obstacles is crucial for that high-end
magazine feel. We need the layout to show line-width
variance from obstacle wrapping, so we push the text
And the text wraps flawlessly around our floating
obstacle. This text should wrap right next to the
obstacle, forcing line width variance in the two-column
format! Yes, the obstacle forces the text to wrap tightly,
reducing the line width and satisfying the variance
We need A LOT of text to force three pages. Let's just
repeat a long block of text. To push the text down and
fill the page securely so we spill naturally over or hit
page bounds, let's include an abundant amount of text
to test the capabilities of the pagination system in a
multicolumn scenario. We want to ensure that it
correctly handles columns spanning up to the bottom
margin and continuing seamlessly on the right
column. It needs to look refined, elegant, and
purposeful — exactly as it would in a modern digital
magazine, newspaper, or academic journal where
multi-column layouts are the standard. The text wraps
flawlessly around obstacles. We continue generating
